REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF J. DENNIS BONNEY AN INDIVIDUAL, AND J. DENNIS BONNEY, TRUSTEE, VIVIAN KENNEDY NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AGAINST DEFENDANT 1810 JACKSON STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION IS DENIED. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE PROVIDED TO SUPPORT THE REQUESTED 3-PART INJUNCTION. PLAINTIFF PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE THAT THE DEFENDANT IS IN THE PROCESS OR PLANNING ANY ACTIONS AS TO ANY OF THE REQUESTS. TO THE EXTENT, PLAINTIFF IS SEEKING SOME TYPE OF A MANDATORY INJUNCTION PERTAINING TO ALREADY RECORDED DOCUMENT, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT SUCH RELIEF MAY BE GRANTED ON A MOTION. FURTHERMORE, PLAINTIFF PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE OF ANY IRREPARABLE HARM THAT CANNOT BE COMPENSATED BY MONEY-DAMAGES SHOULD THE PLAINTIFF PREVAIL IN THIS ACTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-24-2025 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT 1810 JACKSON STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

A motion to strike lies only where the pleading has irrelevant, false or improper matter, or has not been drawn or filed in conformity with laws. (Civ. Proc. Code 436.) A Motion to Strike is the proper procedure for testing the adequacy of a punitive damages allegation because punitive damages are not a cause of action. (Grieves v. Superior Ct. (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 159, 164.) Punitive Damages are recoverable where the defendant has been guilty of oppression, fraud, or malice, express or implied. (Civ. Code 3294.)

The Complaint alleges facts sufficient to state a claim for punitive damages. (See Cmplnt., 7:23-25.) Taking the allegations of the complaint as true, Plaintiff alleges that the Association Board moved up a scheduled vote on the apportionment of common area storage, and did not give notice of the scheduled vote, after being notified of plaintiff's application of ex parte relief to prevent the reapportionment of storage space. This allegation is a sufficient factual pleading demonstrating implied oppression, fraud, or malice. The motion to strike Prayer (page 12, line 6): "Judgment for exemplary damages in the sum to be proven at trial," is denied.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction Against Defendant 1810 Jackson Street Homeowners Association
Off Calendar Dec-09-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF J. DENNIS BONNEY AN INDIVIDUAL, AND J. DENNIS BONNEY, TRUSTEE, VIVIAN KENNEDY NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AGAINST DEFENDANT 1810 JACKSON STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION is DENIED. There is no evidence provided to support the requested 3-part injunction. Plaintiff presents no evidence that the Defendant is in the process or planning any actions as to any of the requests. To the extent, Plaintiff is seeking some type of a mandatory injunction pertaining to already recorded document, Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that such relief may be granted on a motion. Furthermore, Plaintiff presents no evidence of any irreparable harm that cannot be compensated by money-damages should the Plaintiff prevail in this action.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
